# Jamari Anthony Jacquet — Graduation Campaign Kit
### Monarch High School · Home of the Knights · Class of 2026 · Coconut Creek, FL
### "Long Live the Code" 👑💻

> Copy-paste ready. Every link uses the token `{{SITE_URL}}` — swap it for the live celebration site before posting.
> **Visual identity:** Knights **black · red · silver**. Crown + chivalry + terminal energy. Ready-made graphics are in `campaign/assets/` (see Section 11).

---

## 1. Campaign Overview

**Theme:** *Long Live the Code.* Monarch's mascot is the **Knight**, its name means **monarch/crown**, and every classroom carries the **Knights' Code of Chivalry**. The campaign crowns Jamari as a Knight who's now picking up a new code — the kind you write. Graduation = his coronation and his launch.

**Key message (one sentence):**
> Jamari Anthony Jacquet graduated a Monarch Knight, Class of 2026 — and the honor code that made him a Knight is the one he'll build software by.

**Visual identity:** Black, red & silver (real Monarch colors). Crown crest, heraldic lines, metallic silver, a single crimson accent. Cap-and-gown meets a terminal cursor. All graphics in `campaign/assets/`.

**Primary CTA:** Send everyone to the 3D animated celebration site → **{{SITE_URL}}**

**Hashtag strategy:**
- **Primary branded tag:** `#Jamari2026` (every post, every platform)
- **Campaign tag:** `#LongLiveTheCode`
- **School pride:** `#MonarchKnights`
- Rotate supporting tags from the Bank below: 1 primary + 3–6 on IG/TikTok, 1–2 on X, light on LinkedIn.

**Posting rhythm:** Tease → Reveal (grad day) → Knight's Code → Drive to site → Future-dev spotlight. Full 7-day plan in Section 9.

## 3. Instagram

### (a) Feed Post — Carousel Caption (use `carousel-cover.png` as slide 1, photos after)
> Swipe 👉 the crown, the cap, and the kid about to change the game.

```
Long live the Knight. 👑🎓

Jamari Anthony Jacquet — Monarch High, Class of 2026. ✅

Monarch raised him on the Knights' Code: honor, discipline, service. He's carrying every bit of it into what's next — because that same code is what makes a great software developer. The cap and gown? Just the screenshot. The real story is the code he's about to write.

Proud uncle doesn't cover it. Swipe to see our Knight 👉

We built him something special — a whole 3D experience celebrating Jamari. Go leave him a note. Link in bio. 🔗

#Jamari2026 #LongLiveTheCode #ClassOf2026 #MonarchKnights #MonarchHigh #CoconutCreek #FutureSoftwareDeveloper #ProudUncle #WeMadeIt
```

### (b) Reels — Caption + Hook + On-Screen Text

**Hook (first 1.5s):**
> "POV: your nephew got knighted by Monarch High AND already knows what he's building next."

**Caption:**
```
From the Knights' Code to a developer's code. 👑➡️💻

Jamari, Monarch Knights Class of 2026 — just getting started. Full experience in bio. 🔗

#Jamari2026 #LongLiveTheCode #ClassOf2026 #grad2026 #fyp #MonarchKnights #FutureDev
```

**On-screen text beats:**
- 0:00 — `POV: your nephew just graduated 🎓`
- 0:02 — `Monarch Knights • Class of 2026 👑`
- 0:05 — `Coconut Creek raised him 🌴`
- 0:08 — `on the Knights' Code of Chivalry ⚔️`
- 0:11 — `…now watch what he builds 💻`
- 0:14 — `Future software developer. Remember the name.`
- 0:16 — `Jamari Anthony Jacquet → link in bio 🔗`

### (c) Story Frames — use `ig-story-announce.png` for Frame 1
**Frame 1 — Announcement:** post `campaign/assets/ig-story-announce.png` (crest + photo + link pill).
**Frame 2 — Poll:** `Most likely to… build the next big app? 📱` → Poll: "Obviously 💻" / "100% 🚀"
**Frame 3 — Hype:** `The whole family pulled up for this one.` → emoji slider 👑 "How proud are we?"
**Frame 4 — Site Drive:** `We made him a 3D experience 👀 — tap to celebrate Jamari 👇` → Link sticker → {{SITE_URL}}

### (d) Bio-Link Blurb
```
👑 Jamari • Monarch Knights C/O 2026
💻 Future software developer
👇 Celebrate him
{{SITE_URL}}
```

## 4. Facebook

### Longer Heartfelt Post
```
Some moments you wait years for. Today was one of them. 👑🎓

Jamari Anthony Jacquet officially graduated from Monarch High School — Class of 2026, a Monarch Knight through and through.

Monarch raises its kids on the Knights' Code of Chivalry — honor, discipline, service — and Jamari lived it. I remember him pulling me over to show me something he'd figured out on the computer, eyes lit up, talking a hundred miles an hour. That spark never left; it just got sharper. He's not just a graduate. He's a future software developer, and the same code of honor that made him a Knight is exactly what's going to make him a great builder.

To my nephew: we are PROUD of you. Coconut Creek raised a good one. The next chapter is yours to write — or, knowing you, to code. 😄💻

We built something special to celebrate him — a full 3D experience where you can see his story and leave a message. Come show him love:

👉 {{SITE_URL}}

Drop a 👑 in the comments and tell Jamari congratulations. Let's flood it.

#Jamari2026 #LongLiveTheCode #MonarchKnights #ClassOf2026 #CoconutCreek #ProudUncle #FutureSoftwareDeveloper
```

## 6. LinkedIn (classy, door-opening)
```
A proud family moment — and a name worth remembering in tech.

My nephew, Jamari Anthony Jacquet, just graduated from Monarch High School in Coconut Creek, Florida (Class of 2026). Monarch educates its students under a "Knights' Code of Chivalry" — honor, discipline, and service — and Jamari embodies it.

What stands out isn't only the milestone; it's the direction. He's an aspiring software developer with a genuine builder's instinct: the curiosity that turns "how does this work?" into "let me build a better version." That combination — character plus craft — is exactly what the best engineers are made of, and he has it early.

To anyone in software, edtech, or early-talent mentorship: keep an eye on this one. He's emerging talent worth investing in early.

We built a small celebration experience to mark the occasion. If you'd like to leave him an encouraging word, it would mean a lot: {{SITE_URL}}

Congratulations, Jamari. Long live the code. 👑💻

#ClassOf2026 #FutureSoftwareDeveloper #TechTalent #Jamari2026 #MonarchKnights
```

## 7. TikTok

**Video concept:** 15–30s glow-up. Open on young Jamari at a keyboard/controller → on the beat, cut to grad day (cap & gown) with a **crown** graphic dropping onto the frame → final frame flips to a black terminal typing `> long_live_the_code.sh` then the site URL. Black/red/silver grade, silver-and-crimson confetti on the beat drop. Drop the `carousel-cover.png` or `ig-story-announce.png` as the end card.

**Trending-audio:** a triumphant "glow-up / they doubted me, now look" or "main character moment" sound with a clear beat drop ~0:05–0:07 for the cap-and-gown + crown reveal.

**Caption:**
```
From little kid at the keyboard to Monarch Knight 👑🎓➡️💻 Class of 2026, future software developer, our whole heart. Link in bio to celebrate Jamari 🔗 #Jamari2026 #LongLiveTheCode #ClassOf2026 #grad2026 #fyp #MonarchKnights #FutureDev
```

**On-screen beats:**
- 0:00 — `the kid who always wanted to build things…`
- 0:03 — `(always on a screen, always curious 💻)`
- 0:06 — *BEAT DROP — cap & gown + crown* → `MONARCH KNIGHTS • CLASS OF 2026 👑`
- 0:10 — `Coconut Creek made him. 🌴`
- 0:14 — `> long_live_the_code.sh`
- 0:18 — `future software developer 🚀`
- 0:22 — `Jamari Anthony Jacquet`
- 0:26 — `celebrate him → link in bio 🔗`

## 8. The "Blast"

### Group Text / WhatsApp
```
👑🎓 BIG NEWS! Jamari graduated a Monarch Knight — Class of 2026!

We're SO proud. The family built a special 3D celebration site where you can see his story and leave him a message. Takes 30 seconds and means the world:

👉 {{SITE_URL}}

Please share with anyone who loves Jamari. Let's flood his inbox with love. 🎉
```

### Email Blast
**Subject options:**
- `👑 Jamari graduated! Come celebrate our Knight (special site inside)`
- `He did it! Jamari is a Monarch Knight, Class of 2026`
- `Leave Jamari a graduation message — link inside`

**Body:**
```
Hi everyone,

A moment worth celebrating: Jamari Anthony Jacquet just graduated from Monarch High School — Class of 2026, a Monarch Knight! 👑🎓

Those who know Jamari know he's been all about building things and figuring out how everything works — and he's setting his sights on becoming a software developer. Monarch raised him on the Knights' Code of Chivalry, and he's carrying that honor straight into his next chapter. This graduation isn't an ending; it's a launch.

To mark it, we created something special: a 3D animated celebration experience just for Jamari. Explore it, watch his story, and — most importantly — leave him a personal message of congratulations.

Visit the celebration here:
👉 {{SITE_URL}}

It takes less than a minute, and every note will mean the world to him.

Please forward to family and friends who'd want to cheer him on.

With pride and a whole lot of love,
The Jacquet Family

P.S. If you post on social, tag #Jamari2026 so we can find and reshare it! 🎉
```

## 11. Ready-to-Post Image Kit (`campaign/assets/`)

These were generated for this campaign in the real Knights palette (black/red/silver) using Jamari's portrait (duotoned to match) and a custom crown crest. Drop them straight into posts.

| File | Size | Use |
|------|------|-----|
| `carousel-cover.png` | 1080×1080 | Instagram carousel slide 1 / feed hero |
| `card-quote-1.png` | 1080×1080 | "cap & gown is just the screenshot" quote card |
| `card-quote-2.png` | 1080×1080 | "Once a Knight, always a Knight" (Knights' Code) |
| `card-quote-3.png` | 1080×1080 | "Coconut Creek to the codebase" |
| `ig-story-announce.png` | 1080×1920 | Instagram/Facebook Story announcement (has link pill) |
| `share-card.png` | 1200×630 | Link-preview / Open Graph / Twitter card |
| `profile-badge.png` | 512×512 | Profile pic / avatar / WhatsApp DP |

The `ig-story-announce.png` and `share-card.png` contain a `{{SITE_URL}}` placeholder — regenerate or cover with a link sticker after you pick the final URL.

---

## 12. AI-Image Prompts (run in your own pipeline — ChatGPT/DALL·E, MuAPI/Flux, Firefly)

I couldn't call those services from the build sandbox (no outbound network / API keys here), so the kit above was generated in code as a strong baseline. When you want richer, fully-rendered art, run these in your pipeline where the keys live — then send them back and I'll critique + select.

**Global style suffix (append to every prompt):**
> `premium heraldic design, matte black background, crimson red and metallic silver only, dramatic rim lighting, high contrast, clean negative space, no text, no watermark, 4k, editorial.`

**A — Emblem / crest (square, profile + watermark)**
> `A modern heraldic emblem: a sleek silver crown above a minimalist shield bearing a stylized letter "M", small crimson gem accents, fine engraved linework, knightly but contemporary tech-brand feel.` — 1:1. *Negative:* `clutter, gradients, cartoon, photo, faces.`

**B — Hero banner (wide, site/Facebook cover)**
> `Cinematic hero banner for a graduation celebration: a metallic silver crown and a graduation cap merged, floating over a dark stage with silver and crimson confetti and soft volumetric light, a faint terminal/code grid in the background.` — 16:9. *Negative:* `text, logos, people, orange, blue.`

**C — Quote-card background (square, text added later)**
> `Abstract luxury background, black with a single crimson glow in the upper corner, faint diagonal silver pinstripes, subtle knight-armor texture, lots of empty center space for text.` — 1:1. *Negative:* `text, busy, bright colors.`

**D — Stylized portrait treatment (feed it Jamari's grad photo, image-to-image)**
> `Duotone editorial portrait, black shadows to bright silver highlights, single crimson rim light from the right, graduation cap, premium magazine-cover grade, high contrast.` Strength ~0.5–0.65 so his likeness stays intact. *Negative:* `distort face, extra fingers, change identity.`

**Suggested models on MuAPI:** Flux.1.1 Pro (A–C, crisp graphic art), Flux Kontext / an img2img model (D, portrait restyle). On ChatGPT use the image tool for A–C; for D upload his photo and ask for the duotone restyle.
